summary: Fixed inaccurate rollback reporting in TaskService.sync() (taskService.ts:206-236). The catch block now tracks rollbackSuccessCount and rollbackFailureCount separately, and the error message distinguishes created/successful-rollback/failed-rollback counts. Added regression test for partial rollback failure scenario. Verification: bun test (958 pass, 0 fail), warcraft-core build exit 0, biome format clean.

Added `validateUniqueTaskNumbers()` to TaskService that rejects duplicate numbered task headers during sync validation (taskService.ts:716-744), called in `_computeSyncDelta()` before dependency graph validation. The error message lists each duplicate number with both colliding task names. Added 4 regression tests in taskService.sync.test.ts covering single/multiple duplicate groups, error message content, and valid unique-number passthrough. Verification: bun test (29/29 pass across both target files, warcraft-core 962/962 pass), build exit 0, biome check clean on changed files.

Aligned static system prompt tool inventory with runtime registration (17→19). Added warcraft_worktree_prune to Worktree domain and warcraft_doctor in new Diagnostics domain. Added 5 regression tests that extract tool names from both the runtime `tool: {}` block and the prompt table, asserting count=19, no missing tools, no stale tools, and header count matches runtime count. All 21 prompt-consistency tests pass; task/dependency tests unaffected (29 pass); Biome clean.

Created `packages/opencode-warcraft/skills/context7-cli/` with SKILL.md and three reference files (docs.md, skills.md, setup.md) adapted from upstream Context7 CLI skill. MCP-specific setup instructions were removed/rewritten to preserve CLI-only guidance. Ran `bun run generate-skills` (exit 0, 21 skills) which auto-updated `registry.generated.ts` to include the new `context7-cli` entry. Verified: build exit 0, opencode-warcraft tests 778 pass / 0 fail, skills tests 33 pass / 0 fail, no MCP references in local skill content.

Deleted `packages/opencode-warcraft/src/mcp/context7.ts`, removed the `context7` import/entry from `src/mcp/index.ts`, removed the `context7` stanza from `templates/mcp-servers.json`, removed `@upstash/context7-mcp` from `package.json` optionalDependencies, and refreshed `bun.lock` (exit 0, no trace of removed dep). Added `src/mcp/index.test.ts` with 3 tests verifying only `websearch` and `grep_app` remain. Verified: build exit 0, opencode-warcraft tests 781 pass / 0 fail, lint on changed files 0 errors.

Updated 3 documentation files to remove built-in Context7 MCP references: (1) README.md — removed `context7` from architecture tree, `@upstash/context7-mcp` from dependency list and troubleshooting install command; (2) packages/opencode-warcraft/README.md — removed `context7_query-docs` from MCP template paragraph (it implied the tool came from built-in MCP), removed `context7` row from Available MCPs table; (3) packages/opencode-warcraft/AGENTS.md — updated `src/mcp/` description to list only `websearch` and `grep-app`. Preserved all other Context7 docs-tooling mentions that don't claim built-in MCP origin. Verified: all 4 acceptance grep searches return 0 hits, build exit 0.

Final package-level validation passed end-to-end. Added context7-cli regression test to skill-content.test.ts (782 pass / 0 fail, up from 781). Build: exit 0 (21 skills). TypeScript typecheck: exit 0. Biome lint: 0 errors on changed files (208 pre-existing errors in warcraft-core are unrelated). Stale reference searches confirmed: 0 hits for `https://mcp.context7.com/mcp`, `@upstash/context7-mcp`, and `context7-mcp` in bun.lock. New context7-cli skill confirmed in registry.generated.ts and skill source.

Ran `npx biome check . --write` from repo root, which auto-fixed 157 files (formatting, import ordering, safe lint fixes). Biome exited 0; residual diagnostics are 78 warnings + 6 infos — all unsafe fixes (noUnusedVariables, noUnusedImports, useTemplate) documented in context file `task1-residual-diagnostics` for Task 2. Verification: build exit 0, lint exit 0, warcraft-core tests 4095/4101 pass (6 pre-existing worktree-sensitive failures in detection tests, not caused by Biome changes).

Applied `npx biome check . --write --unsafe` to resolve all 78 warnings + 6 infos (84 residual diagnostics) across 63 test files. Changes are purely mechanical: removed unused imports (noUnusedImports), prefixed unused variables with underscore (noUnusedVariables), converted string concatenation to template literals (useTemplate), and replaced computed string keys with literal keys (useLiteralKeys). Verified: `npx biome check .` exits 0 with 0 warnings, 0 infos, 0 errors. All 63 changed files were in the original Biome residual diagnostic output. Build failure in opencode-warcraft package is pre-existing (module resolution in worktree), not caused by these changes.
